We are given two angles and a non-included side (AAS). This defines a single, unique triangle.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } B.

\angle B = 180^\circ - \angle A - \angle C

\angle B = 180^\circ - 42^\circ - 64^\circ = 74.0^\circ

\text{Step 2: Use the Law of Sines to find side } b.

\frac{b}{\sin(B)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

b = \frac{12.5\sin(74^\circ)}{\sin(42^\circ)} \approx 17.96

\text{Step 3: Use the Law of Sines to find side } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

c = \frac{12.5\sin(64^\circ)}{\sin(42^\circ)} \approx 16.79
